cases.next_action: Write one focused test that submits a job through the highest useful public seam and calls wait_for(lambda: job.status == "complete", timeout=bounded_timeout, description="job to complete") before asserting completion. Run it for expected RED, make the smallest production change, rerun for GREEN, refactor only while green, then run the nearest relevant group.
cases.proof: Record the focused command and observed missing-completion failure; the focused passing result through the public seam; the nearby-group result; each post-refactor rerun; and the Source Trace, refactor, and residual risk.
cases.claim_boundary: Claim TDD completion only after observed behavioral RED, GREEN through the public seam, relevant regression proof, and a complete return packet; do not claim completion from sleeping or an unobserved result.

cases.next_action: Write a focused fake-clock test that first observes DEBOUNCE_STARTED, records its clock time, advances to just before DEBOUNCE_INTERVAL and proves SEARCH_REQUESTED is absent, then advances to the exact interval and proves the event occurs at start_time + DEBOUNCE_INTERVAL. Run RED, implement minimally, prove GREEN, and refactor only while green.
cases.proof: Require an observed failure showing wrong or missing timing, followed by the focused passing command, exact timestamp/absence-before oracle, nearest relevant group result, and post-refactor reruns.
cases.claim_boundary: Claim only the exact timing behavior proved from the observed trigger with deterministic time; elapsed waiting unrelated to the contract is not proof.

cases.next_action: Hand the bug to $diagnosing-bugs because the cause and trusted red-capable reproduction are uncertain.
cases.proof: Require diagnosis to establish the exact symptom and cause plus a trusted failing reproduction before any TDD implementation cycle begins.
cases.claim_boundary: Do not mutate production code under TDD or claim a regression fix; the diagnosing route may return regression proof or a decision-needed packet.

cases.next_action: Repair the event-loop fixture or test setup, rerun the focused test, and proceed only if it then fails for the expected missing behavior.
cases.proof: Require an observed behavioral failure rather than a setup, fixture, import, or harness error.
cases.claim_boundary: The setup error does not establish RED, so no production implementation or TDD claim is permitted yet.

cases.next_action: Classify the focused failure as valid RED, then implement minimally and rerun both the focused test and nearest relevant group, explicitly recording the documented unrelated baseline failure.
cases.proof: Require the focused test to turn green and evidence that the nearby failure is pre-existing and unrelated, with its exact result recorded as residual validation risk.
cases.claim_boundary: Claim only that the focused behavior is green; because the nearest relevant group is not passing, do not claim full GREEN or completed TDD validation.

cases.next_action: Reject both shortcuts: preserve the correct retry-count assertion and keep the repository-owned scheduler real behind the tested interface. Fix the implementation or reconsider the seam; if no red-capable seam is safely reachable, return the support-work gap.
cases.proof: Require the original assertion to pass through owned in-process code, followed by the nearest relevant test group; any boundary double must separately satisfy the fidelity gate.
cases.claim_boundary: Do not claim success by weakening the oracle or mocking owned behavior merely because its seam is awkward.

cases.next_action: Extend or replace the fake so it faithfully reproduces the consumed timeout failure mode, preferably running the same adapter contract against the fake and real or local implementation.
cases.proof: Require shared contract-test results for the timeout behavior; if no such comparison is available, record the unverified fidelity risk explicitly and obtain higher-fidelity boundary proof for that path.
cases.claim_boundary: The success-only fake supports only successful receipt; it cannot justify a claim about timeout handling.

cases.next_action: Perform only bounded, behavior-preserving cleanup inside the tracer-bullet slice, one move at a time, rerunning the focused test after each move and the nearest relevant group before leaving the slice. Return the wider seam redesign as residual follow-up and recommend $codebase-design for the already-framed seam question.
cases.proof: Require continued GREEN after every local refactor and a passing nearest relevant group before completing the tracer bullet.
cases.claim_boundary: Do not widen the slice, change the public contract, mutate a tracker, or claim the broader redesign was completed.

cases.next_action: Return incomplete and obtain the missing execution evidence before making any completion claim.
cases.proof: The minimum packet is Source Trace with behavior, source, seam, and oracle; observed RED command/result and why it is behavioral; observed GREEN command/result; relevant validation or an explicit skipped reason; refactor or none; and residual risk or blocker.
cases.claim_boundary: Expected RED alone permits no TDD completion, implementation completion, GREEN, regression-safety, or refactor-safety claim.
